channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says he urged Israel, Hezbollah to hold fire as tensions in Lebanon rise
item.description: President Trump says he asked Israel to delay attacking Beirut and urged Hezbollah to hold fire. The effort comes as Iran halts talks with the U.S. amid Israel's expanding invasion of Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says he asked Israel to delay attacking Beirut and urged Hezbollah to hold fire. The effort comes as Iran halts talks with the U.S. amid Israel's expanding invasion of Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn., shares his thoughts on the U.S.-Iran peace talks
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Democratic Rep. Jim Himes of Connecticut, ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee, for his view of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and negotiations.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Democratic Rep. Jim Himes of Connecticut, ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee, for his view of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Iranians back online after a government-imposed blackout
item.description: People in Iran are back online after a government-imposed digital blackout that lasted nearly three months. Reconnecting has been bittersweet for many Iranians.
item.content:encoded: People in Iran are back online after a government-imposed digital blackout that lasted nearly three months. Reconnecting has been bittersweet for many Iranians.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: DOJ says it will pause its 'anti-weaponization' fund after judge's ruling, Trump says he urged Israel, Hezbollah to hold fire amid rising tensions over Lebanon, Californians vote in state's primaries.
item.content:encoded: DOJ says it will pause its 'anti-weaponization' fund after judge's ruling, Trump says he urged Israel, Hezbollah to hold fire amid rising tensions over Lebanon, Californians vote in state's primaries.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 'Why not me?': André De Shields on his Tony nomination and a life in the theatre
item.description: Broadway actor André De Shields discusses his Tony-nominated performance in CATS: The Jellicle Ball, turning 80 and his philosophy on life.
item.content:encoded:
Broadway actor André De Shields discusses his Tony-nominated performance in CATS: The Jellicle Ball, turning 80 and his philosophy on life.

(Image credit: Matthew Murphy and Evan Zimmerman )
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: With its return home, major plumbing issue on board USS Ford can be addressed
item.description: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.content:encoded: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Political parties once avoided primaries. In many places, that's changed
item.description: For years, political parties in most states have avoided getting involved in primary races -- leaving the choice to voters. But in many places, those elections are looking different this year.
item.content:encoded: For years, political parties in most states have avoided getting involved in primary races -- leaving the choice to voters. But in many places, those elections are looking different this year.
item.dc:creator: Shaylee Ragar

item.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
item.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.content:encoded: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: In Oklahoma, unused land at prisons becomes a pollinator's paradise
item.description: Pollinator gardens help migrating birds and butterflies. One state on their flight path is planting habitats at its prisons.
item.content:encoded: Pollinator gardens help migrating birds and butterflies. One state on their flight path is planting habitats at its prisons.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Bennett-Steele

item.title: Justice Department says it will pause 'anti-weaponization' fund after judge's ruling
item.description: The Justice Department says it will abide by a federal judge's ruling pausing the government's creation of a $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund that has drawn bipartisan pushback in Congress.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department says it will abide by a federal judge's ruling pausing the government's creation of a $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund that has drawn bipartisan pushback in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore
